items.title:
A good meeting with the leaders of communities in the Kyiv and Chernihiv regions. I thank everyone for the substantive work and for caring so much about your communities, your people, and all of our Ukraine.

We discussed protection against potential Russian threats in the Chernihiv-Kyiv direction, as well as strengthening air defense and protection against drones in the north of Ukraine. The Ministry of Defense has been given the relevant task.

The threats that existed from the first days of the full-scale war remain just as present today. Everyone understands perfectly well that Ukraine cannot allow even a fraction of a repeat of the risks our people faced back in February 2022. The implementation of resilience plans, the strengthening of the defense of the Chernihiv-Kyiv direction, the reinforcement of fortifications in this direction, and the number and training of personnel – all these signals from us reduce the chance of renewed escalation.

We also spoke separately about repairing local roads. Logistics is extremely important, and all work must be carried out as soon as possible. Our government officials have also been tasked with providing greater support to the Chornobyl NPP staff. The Minister of Finance of Ukraine promised that decisions on the issue of salaries would be made already in June.

I thank every community for its active engagement. We will continue this format of interaction with communities and the prompt resolution of issues.

items.title: Home Buyers Hit as Bond Rout Drives Rates Higher
items.summary:
Since the oil-price shock from President Donald Trump’s Iran war unleashed the biggest jump in inflation since 2023, bond prices have tumbled, pushing yields on some US government debt to the highest levels in nearly two decades. 
The rate on 10-year Treasuries, which sets the floor for mortgages, has climbed to around 4.6%, with traders saying 5% is within reach.
Gennadiy Goldberg, Head of US Rat

items.title: Slashing the Gas Tax Will Save Drivers 18 Cents Per Gallon, Agen Says
items.summary: Jarrod Agen, Executive Director of the National Energy Dominance Council, talks about how the White House is trying to drive down gasoline prices. He says the "war on oil and gas" is over. Agen says slashing the federal gas tax would save drivers 18 cents per gallon. He speaks on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title:
RT by @mfa_russia: Statement by Russian Permanent Representative Dmitry #Polyanskiy at the OSCE, 21 May 2026, on Western efforts to undermine a peaceful settlement in Ukraine

• If the OSCE had a Doomsday Clock, its hands would have to move closer to midnight. Lithuania's FM Budrys openly called on NATO to attack Kaliningrad: "NATO has the means to destroy Russian bases." European elites have moved to direct calls for war against Russia.

• Russia's Foreign Intelligence Service reports: Kiev is planning sabotage attacks from Latvia — drone launches. Ukrainian operators are already at Latvian bases: Ādaži, Sēlija, Lielvārde, Daugavpils, Jēkabpils. Our response will be decisive. Launch sites will be identified.

• NATO's "Narrow Waters 26-1" exercises are underway in the Gulf of Finland — at the entrance to Russia's territorial waters. Alliance military activity near our borders has reached Cold War levels. A blockade of Kaliningrad will lead to unprecedented escalation. Don't say you weren't warned.

• Ukrainian naval drones have reached Greece. A Ukrainian drone was neutralized near the island of Lefkada. Greece tried to hush up the attack on the tanker Maran Homer. Now the terrorists are at your doorstep. Is this what you wanted?

• Russia is open to dialogue — to a serious conversation about security guarantees. But the West sees our openness as weakness. The response is sanctions and weapons. Our patience is not unlimited.

• On 12 May, Russia successfully test-launched the "Sarmat" ICBM. On 18 May, joint Russian-Belarusian nuclear exercises began. This is not a threat — this is deterrence. If NATO continues to increase military activity near our borders, we will further strengthen our defence capabilities.

• There is little cause for optimism. You do not want to hear calls for common sense. In Beijing, Russia and China called for dialogue to resolve the crisis. You give us no reason to expect prudence from you.

Read the full text: http://clck.ru/3Tm339

items.title: Jet Fuel’s War Surge Vindicates Airlines Who Stuck With Hedging
items.summary: The surge in the cost of jet fuel since the start of the Iran war is highlighting the vast disparity in how well individual airlines protect themselves against a price spike.

items.title: Nuclear Company Deep Fission Files for $156 Million IPO
items.summary: Deep Fission Inc. is seeking to raise up to $156 million in a US initial public offering, the latest nuclear startup to go that route as artificial intelligence data centers stoke demand for electricity.

items.title: How Bond Yield Surge Will Impact Economies, Markets
items.summary: A prolonged period of elevated long-term bond yields is ramping up borrowing costs around the world. That’s because investors are demanding more and more compensation for holding government debt after the war in Iran ignited fresh inflation fears and concerns also swirl about the level of public debt and central bank interest rates.

I had a phone call with the Prime Minister of Estonia, @KristenMichalPM. We discussed preparations for the NB8 format with Ukraine, which will take place in Estonia – we hope it will be truly productive. I thanked Estonia for the support it has provided since the first days of the full-scale invasion and informed Kristen about the situation on the frontline. I am grateful for his kind words about our warriors. Ukraine’s position on the battlefield is now indeed stronger than in previous years. We also discussed diplomatic and security prospects. We agreed to continue working substantively on all issues, including security-related ones. Thank you!

RT by @UN: .@volker_turk deplores the execution of two more people in Iran. Since the military escalation began, at least 34 people have been executed on political & national security charges, including in the context of the January protests. It is unacceptable that the Iranian authorities are instrumentalising the current conflict to further stifle dissent. The UN Human Rights Chief urges Iranian authorities to promptly impose a total moratorium on the death penalty.

items.title: Brazilian Executives Waver on Flávio Bolsonaro Over His Ties to Jailed CEO
items.summary: Some Brazilian executives and investors are tempering their support for presidential candidate Flávio Bolsonaro as he seeks backing in the nation’s financial hub days after details emerged about his relationship with a scandal-plagued banker.

items.title: Oil Gains as Iran’s Uranium Stance Clouds Hormuz Outlook
items.summary:
Oil gained Monday as traders weighed conflicting reports on Iran’s uranium enrichment, a key sticking point in peace negotiations with the US that could dictate the pace of reopening the vital Strait of Hormuz.
